name: Dr. Mamdouh Salama
shortBio: Dr. Mamdouh Salama is a distinguished scholar who combines academic excellence with a deep commitment to Islamic education. A graduate of MIT and a Life Fellow of ASME, he has dedicated decades to delivering Quranic lectures and writing about Islam.
fullBio:
Dr. Mamdouh Salama holds Bachelor and Master of Science degrees in Production Engineering from Ain-Shams University, Egypt, and Master of Science and Doctor of Science degrees in Mechanical Engineering from Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT).

He is a Life Fellow of the American Society of Mechanical Engineers (ASME) and has served as chairman of several ASME conferences and editor for several conference proceedings. Dr. Salama is well recognized in his technical field and the recipient of several achievement awards. He has published more than 125 technical papers and holds 25 patents.

In the religious arena, Dr. Salama has written a book entitled "Your Reflection on the Mirror of Islam" and has published several articles about Islam in local newspapers. He has given many lectures to both Muslim and Non-Muslim groups on the subject of Islam.

In addition, he has prepared several other books that have not been published yet, including:

• "Reflection on the Theme of the Chapters of the Qur'an"
• "Journey through the Shoreless Ocean of the Meaning of Surat Al-Baqara"
• "The Myth of Abrogation (Naskh) in the Quran"
• "The Memoirs of the Prophet (PBUH)"
• "Reflections on Selected Verses of Qur'an"
• "The Companions of the Prophet (PBUH)"
• "Divine Communications to the Believers"
• "Basis of Fiqh"
• "Foundational Principles for Verifying Islamic Information"
education: B.Sc. in Production Engineering — Ain-Shams University, Egypt
M.Sc. in Production Engineering — Ain-Shams University, Egypt
M.Sc. in Mechanical Engineering — Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT)
